figures.caption: Fig. 8: SHAP beeswarm plot for the cartpole domain. Panels as in Figure 6.
Fig. 9: SHAP beeswarm plot for the hexapod domain. Panels as in Figure 6.

Fig. 1: Illustration of the MONET task network structure. Each node Vi = τ i represents a task, labelled by its current best solution A[τ i] ∈ Θ. (a) Task network with the individual and social learning strategies that operate on it. (b) Task network and solution qualities at 500,000 evaluations (50% of the total).
Table 1: Final mean fitness and AUC (×103 on the [0, 1] domains) per domain. Mean ± std over 20 random seeds per (algorithm, domain) pair.

Fig. 6: SHAP beeswarm plot for the archery domain. Each row is a hyperparameter; each dot is a MONET run. Color indicates the feature value (red = high, blue = low); horizontal position indicates the SHAP value.
Table 5: Top 10 MONET configurations by combined z-score (ndomains ≥ 2).
Fig. 5: Global hyperparameter importance for MONET: mean absolute SHAP value on a gradient-boosted tree surrogate.
Fig. 4: Algorithm comparison across the four benchmark domains.
Fig. 7: SHAP beeswarm plot for the arm domain. Panels as in Figure 6.
Fig. 2: Commutative diagrams comparing MAP-Elites and MONET.
